rag_rules: Use AGREE syntax: assume/guarantee for contracts, eq for equations, const for constants, assign for assignments
Place component type contracts in type scopes, implementation contracts in implementation scopes
Use initialized temporal recurrence for stateful equations
Use nested if ... then ... else for branching logic
Use AGREE logical operators (and, or, not, =>) for contracts
generic_rules: Place input envelope assumptions and output range guarantees only when concrete bounds or visible relations are available
Use exact visible identifiers from the current component scope
Do not encode AADL timing properties as AGREE expressions unless explicitly exposed as ports or constants
Use AGREE scalar types (int, real, bool) for declarations
Delete or rewrite AGREE clauses when unresolved references are reported
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use exact visible identifiers from the current component scope
Use AGREE scalar types (int, real, bool) for declarations
Use initialized temporal recurrence for stateful equations
Use nested if ... then ... else for branching logic
anti_patterns: Do not invent declarations for unresolved references
Do not encode AADL timing properties as AGREE expressions without explicit ports or constants
Do not use AADL property names directly in AGREE expressions
Do not use invalid AGREE scalar type names (e.g., integer, boolean)
